title: calamagrostis angustifolia subsp. tenuis
intro: Calamagrostis angustifolia subsp. tenuis, commonly known as slender reed grass or slender fescue grass, is a subspecies of grass native to various regions of the world, particularly in Asia and Europe. This grass has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Calamagrostis angustifolia subsp. tenuis include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
